reasoning: Total imports from Russia in 2024 were approximately 52 bcm, averaging 13 bcm/quarter. Q1 2024 was less than 14 bcm, meaning even before the Ukraine transit ended, quarters were capped below 15 bcm. The expiration of the Ukraine pipeline route in January 2025 removed roughly 3.5 bcm/quarter of capacity. Remaining imports rely on TurkStream (averaging ~5 bcm/quarter) and LNG (~6.7 bcm/quarter according to February 2025 run-rates). Combined, these routes suggest a typical 2025 quarter will import around 11.7 bcm. To hit the 15 bcm threshold, LNG would need to surge to nearly 10 bcm/quarter (40 bcm annualized), which approaches total Russian global LNG capacity and would exceed European regasification allocations for Russian gas. Without massive capacity additions or political reversals, reaching 15 bcm in any quarter is highly improbable.
batch_queries: Will the EU import at least 15 billion cubic meters of natural gas from Russia in any quarter of 2025?
